constants.name: Speed of light in vacuum
constants.usage: Relativity, electromagnetism, all of optics. E = mc². Pioneer 0's radio signal traveled at c.

constants.name: Planck constant
constants.usage: Quantum mechanics, photon energy E = hf. Every photon detected by a NASA instrument carries energy quantized by h.

constants.name: Reduced Planck constant
constants.usage: ℏ = h/(2π). Appears in angular momentum quantization, Schrödinger equation, Heisenberg uncertainty.

constants.name: Gravitational constant
constants.usage: Newton's law of gravitation: F = GMm/r². Every orbital mechanics calculation in the NASA series. The least precisely known fundamental constant.

constants.name: Boltzmann constant
constants.usage: Connects temperature to energy. Thermal noise in electronics, atmospheric modeling, blackbody radiation.

constants.name: Elementary charge
constants.usage: Charge of proton (+e) and electron (-e). Every circuit, every LED, every Van Allen belt measurement.

constants.name: Avogadro constant
constants.usage: Number of particles per mole. Connects atomic mass to macroscopic mass. Propellant chemistry, life support.

constants.name: Electron mass
constants.usage: Electron behavior in detectors, plasma physics, Van Allen belt charged particle dynamics.

constants.name: Proton mass
constants.usage: Nuclear physics, cosmic ray interactions, radiation shielding calculations.

constants.name: Neutron mass
constants.usage: Nuclear physics, neutron activation analysis (Apollo lunar samples).

constants.name: Fine-structure constant
constants.usage: α ≈ 1/137. Strength of electromagnetic interaction. Governs atomic spectra — every spectroscopy mission.

constants.name: Rydberg constant
constants.usage: Hydrogen spectral lines. Most precisely measured physical constant. Spectroscopy calibration.

constants.name: Stefan-Boltzmann constant
constants.usage: Total power radiated by a blackbody: P = σAT⁴. Spacecraft thermal design, star luminosity.

constants.name: Wien displacement constant
constants.usage: Peak wavelength of blackbody: λ_max = b/T. Determines what color a hot object glows.

constants.name: Vacuum permittivity
constants.usage: Coulomb's law, capacitance, electromagnetic wave propagation. Every radio link in spaceflight.

constants.name: Vacuum permeability
constants.usage: Magnetic field calculations, inductance, electromagnetic wave propagation. c² = 1/(ε₀μ₀).

constants.name: Standard acceleration of gravity
constants.usage: Specific impulse definition: Isp = F/(ṁg₀). Every rocket equation calculation in the series.

constants.name: Standard atmosphere
constants.usage: Cabin pressure reference. Life support systems. Atmospheric reentry calculations.

constants.name: Gas constant
constants.usage: Ideal gas law PV = nRT. Propellant thermodynamics, atmospheric modeling, combustion chemistry.

constants.name: Astronomical unit
constants.usage: Earth-Sun distance. Standard distance unit for solar system navigation.

constants.name: Light-year
constants.usage: Distance light travels in one Julian year. Interstellar distance reference.

constants.name: Parsec
constants.usage: 1 AU at 1 arcsecond parallax. Professional astronomy distance unit. 1 pc ≈ 3.26 ly.

constants.name: Solar mass
constants.usage: Standard mass unit for stars, galaxies. GM☉ is known far more precisely than either G or M☉ alone.

constants.name: Solar luminosity
constants.usage: Standard luminosity unit. Solar panel design, spacecraft thermal environment at distance r: flux = L☉/(4πr²).

constants.name: Earth mass
constants.usage: Orbital mechanics. GM⊕ = 3.986004418e14 m³/s² (standard gravitational parameter, known very precisely).

constants.name: Earth equatorial radius
constants.usage: Orbital altitude reference. Atmospheric reentry corridor calculations.

constants.name: Moon mass
constants.usage: Lunar orbital mechanics. Pioneer 0's target. GM☽ = 4.9048695e12 m³/s².

constants.name: Moon mean radius
constants.usage: Lunar landing trajectory calculations, horizon distance, surface gravity (1.625 m/s²).
